# Copilot Instructions for fixated

## Project Overview

`fixated` is an R package. This repository is in early development.

## Repository Structure

This is a standard R package repository. As the package develops, it will follow this structure:

```
fixated/
├── .github/                  # GitHub configuration and workflows
├── R/                        # R source files
├── tests/                    # Test files (testthat framework)
│   └── testthat/
├── man/                      # Auto-generated documentation (roxygen2)
├── vignettes/                # Long-form documentation
├── DESCRIPTION               # Package metadata
├── NAMESPACE                 # Exported functions (auto-generated by roxygen2)
└── README.md                 # Package overview
```

## Development Environment

- **Language**: R
- **Documentation**: roxygen2 (inline documentation with `#'` comments)
- **Testing**: testthat
- **Build**: Standard R package build tools (`devtools`, `usethis`)

## Building and Testing

```r
# Install dependencies
devtools::install_deps()

# Run all tests
devtools::test()

# Check the package (CRAN-level checks)
devtools::check()

# Build documentation
devtools::document()
```

## Coding Conventions

- Follow the [tidyverse style guide](https://style.tidyverse.org/) for R code
- Document all exported functions using roxygen2 comments
- Write tests using the `testthat` framework for all new functions
- Use `snake_case` for function and variable names
- Keep functions focused and small; prefer many small functions over large monolithic ones
- Use the `usethis` package to add new R scripts, tests, and other standard package components

## Adding New Features

When adding new R functions:
1. Create or edit the appropriate `.R` file in the `R/` directory
2. Add roxygen2 documentation above the function
3. Run `devtools::document()` to update the `NAMESPACE` and `man/` files
4. Write corresponding tests in `tests/testthat/`
5. Run `devtools::test()` to verify all tests pass
6. Run `devtools::check()` to ensure no R CMD check warnings or errors

## Pull Request Guidelines

- Keep changes small and focused
- Include tests for all new functionality
- Ensure `devtools::check()` passes with no errors or warnings before submitting
- Update `NEWS.md` (when it exists) with a brief description of changes
